---
title: "Teaching and Training"
---

# Lecture 4 - Correlation and Simple Linear Regression (Dec 4, 2025)

In this **StatsClinic R Lab** taught by **Daniel Olofin**, participants are introduced to correlation and simple linear regression using real clinical and public health datasets.

## Topics Covered

### Correlation
- Visualizing relationships between variables  
- Computing **Pearson correlation**  
- Understanding correlation strength and direction  

### Simple Linear Regression
- Fitting regression models with **lm()**  
- Introduction to **glm()** for generalized linear models  
- Interpreting:
  - Regression coefficients  
  - p-values  
  - R-squared  
  - Residual diagnostics (checking model assumptions)  

### Additional Concepts
- Using **binary, categorical, and multi-variable predictors** in R  
- Comparing models using **AIC**  
- How these concepts fit into the **Linear Regression module** in the StatsClinic November course series  

**Materials:**  
- [Video Link](https://youtu.be/SRApJDD3RqU?si=r0wokPxmpnlBbva8)


# Lecture 3 - T-Tests and Non-Parametric Alternatives (Nov 23, 2025)

In this class, **Daniel Olofin** takes students through key statistical hypothesis-testing methods, including:

## T-Tests
- **One-sample t-test**  
- **Paired t-test**  
- **Independent samples t-test**
- **ANOVA**

## Non-Parametric Alternatives
- **Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test** (paired/one-sample)  
- **Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test** (independent samples)

These methods are introduced with practical examples and guidance on when to choose parametric vs. non-parametric approaches.

**Materials:**  
- [Video Link 1](https://youtu.be/FlxNob4WefU?si=KPdUPhWpLelO84nj)

- [Vidoe Link 2](https://youtu.be/dweje4fXXAU?si=tIswqF78fGYkMJxi)

# Lecture 2 - Introduction to R and RStudio at StatsClinic (Nov 9, 2025)

In this live session, **Daniel Olofin** introduces the basics of using **R**, including:

- Installing and setting up the software  
- Understanding the R and RStudio interface  
- Navigating the Console, Script Editor, Environment, and Files/Plots/Tabs  
- Writing and running your first lines of R code  

*This session is designed to help complete beginners get comfortable with the R environment and begin coding with confidence.*

**Materials:**  
- [Video Link](https://youtu.be/1QOuUYzqztk?si=CLP9trGlDGe-vVBy)


# Lecture 1 - Introduction to Descriptive Statistics (R4DataScience)

A hands-on tutorial introducing key concepts in **descriptive statistics** and **statistical inference** using **R**—designed to build a strong foundation for analyzing data in research contexts at **R4DataScience**.

Participants learn how to summarize data, compute common descriptive measures, visualize distributions, and begin working with R syntax for basic statistical analysis.

**Materials:**  
- [Video Link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1jldVQlu6QmWFpEEX1RWY9ukYDiDhBdr-/view?usp=drive_link)
